pairs.question: What is the Kraft Heinz Company and when was it formed?
pairs.answer: The Kraft Heinz Company (KHC) is an American multinational food company created by the merger of Kraft Foods Group, Inc. and the H.J. Heinz Company. The merger was completed in 2015.

pairs.question: What are the major brands under Kraft Heinz besides Kraft and Heinz?
pairs.answer: Kraft Heinz owns over 20 other brands including Boca Burger, Gevalia, Grey Poupon, Oscar Mayer, Philadelphia Cream Cheese, Primal Kitchen, and Wattie's.

pairs.question: How did the Kraft Heinz split into two companies work?
pairs.answer: On September 2, 2025, Kraft Heinz announced a planned split into two companies: Global Taste Elevation Co. (focused on condiments) and North American Grocery Co. (focused on grocery staples). The split was scheduled for completion in the second half of 2026, but was paused on February 11, 2026.

pairs.question: Who was appointed as the new CEO of Kraft Heinz in December 2025?
pairs.answer: Steve Cahillane was named the new CEO of Kraft Heinz in December 2025, succeeding former CEO Carlos Abrams-Rivera who will serve as an advisor until March 6, 2026.

pairs.question: What was Kraft Heinz's annual sales figure as of 2021?
pairs.answer: As of 2021, Kraft Heinz reported over $26.0 billion in annual sales.

pairs.question: Why was the planned split of Kraft Heinz paused in February 2026?
pairs.answer: On February 11, 2026, Kraft Heinz announced that the planned split into Global Taste Elevation Co. and North American Grocery Co. had been paused, though the reason was not specified in the provided information.

pairs.question: How did Kraft Heinz rank in the 2018 Fortune 500 list?
pairs.answer: Kraft Heinz ranked 114th in the 2018 Fortune 500 list of the largest U.S. corporations based on 2017 total revenue.
